name: delos-unichunking

metadata.name: delos-unichunking
metadata.summary: Universal chunking functions to extract LLM-friendly chunks from any file type.

documentation:
# Unichunking

Extract LLM-friendly chunks from any file type.

Supported file types are :

 - DOCX & DOCX-like (DOC, ODT)
 - PPTX & PPTX-like (PPT, ODP)
 - XLSX & XLSX-like (XLS, ODS)
 - TXT, MD, CSV
 - IPYNB

# Installation

To install, run the following command:

```bash
python3 -m pip install delos-unichunking
```

# How to use

The main functions are :

 - `extract_subchunks` returns a list of all the text particles in the file.
 - `split_chunks_with_overlap` transforms a list of subchunks on a given page into a list of chunks following default or specified parameters for minimum/maximum token size and overlap.
 - `build_chunked_pages` returns a list of "pages", which are lists of formated chunks, following the structure of the document.
 - `compute_pages` approximates the pagination of a file that does not have a native pagination system (such as DOCX) by comparing it to a PDF version.

# Specificities

Please note that the package requires a LibreOffice installation to run `soffice` commands, used during file conversions : for instance, DOC/ODT are first converted to DOCX format and processed as such.

The page numbers computed for DOCX files are an approximation and can be off by a few pages for large files.

Artifical page numbers are used for page-less structures such as TXT files, or to split large XLSX sheets into multiple pages, to follow a tokens-per-page limit.

params.name: str | int | dict[str, dict[str, float] | list[dict[str, float]]]

documentation: Converts a Chunk object to a dictionary.

Class for storing a piece of text and associated metadata.

Fields:
    - chunk_num: The number of chunk inside the page.
    - content: Text of chunk.
    - metadata: {
            page : Number of page to which it belongs,
            file_name: Name of file,
            positions: Positions of all SubChunks inside the Chunk,
            bbox: Coordinates of the minimal bounding box for the Chunk, only exists if computed with dedicated function,
        },
    - embedding: Embedding of chunk text (not handled by unichunking package).
    - chunk_uid: Unique identifier of the chunk.
    - file_hash: File unique reference.
    - num_tokens: Number of tokens.

documentation: Returns a string representing the table/chart with its title and content.

methods.name: unichunking.types.matrix_table.MatrixTable.read_as_str
documentation: Returns a string containing all the text in the table, without any structure.

Class for handling tables and charts in a functional structure, allowing algorithmic correction and reading.

Fields:
    title: Title of the table, empty by default.
    content: Matrix representation of the table content.
    is_chart: Boolean marker indicating whether the data table must be read as a chart.

modules.name: unichunking.tools.file_converter
documentation: Converts files to Office format or PDF.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.file_converter.convert_file
documentation:
Converts DOC/XLS/PPT and ODT/ODS/ODP to DOCX/XLSX/PPTX or PDF.

Args:
    input_path: Path to the local file to convert.
    extension: Output format.

Returns:
    The path to the local converted file.

modules.name: unichunking.tools
documentation: Various useful tools for multi-format file processing.

modules.name: unichunking.tools.markdown_interpreter
documentation: Reads MarkDown and identifies tables.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.markdown_interpreter.md_interpreter
documentation:
Reads MarkDown with the ability to identify tables.

Args:
    lines: Lines of text extracted from file.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.

modules.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning
documentation: Auxiliary functions to clean the text in a chunk.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._clean_extra_whitespace
documentation:
Cleans extra whitespace characters that appear between words.

Example:
-------
ITEM 1.     BUSINESS -> ITEM 1. BUSINESS

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._clean_bullets
documentation:
Cleans unicode bullets from a section of text.

Example:
-------
●  This is an excellent point! -> This is an excellent point!

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._clean
documentation: Cleans text.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._clean_ligatures
documentation:
Replaces ligatures with their most likely equivalent characters.

Example:
-------
The beneﬁts -> The benefits
High quality ﬁnancial -> High quality financial

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._replace_unicode_quotes
documentation:
Replaces unicode bullets in text with the expected character.

Example:
-------
\x93What a lovely quote!\x94 -> “What a lovely quote!”

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning._clean_non_utf8_chars
documentation:
Cleans characters other than UTF-8 from a string.

Example:
-------
b'\xc2\x88This text contains non-ascii characters!\xc2\x88'
    -> This text contains non-ascii characters!

functions.name: unichunking.tools.chunk_cleaning.clean_chunk
documentation: Remove unwanted characters/blanks for better LLM readability.

modules.name: unichunking.tools.xml_tools
documentation: Functions to extract data from XML structures.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.xml_tools._extract_charts_xml
documentation:
Extracts the XML encoding of a chart in a DOCX/PPTX file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the local file.
    element_xml: XML encoding of the element pointing to the chart in the file.
    extension: File extension.
    page_idx: Index of the page containing the chart in the file.

Returns:
    A list of length 1 (for compatibility reasons) containing the XML encoding of the chart.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.xml_tools.extract_charts
documentation:
Extracts the data of Office charts from their XML encoding.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the local file.
    element_xml: XML encoding of the element pointing to the chart in the file.
    extension: File extension.
    page_idx: Index of the page containing the chart in the file.
    charts_xml: A list containing the XML encodings of all charts to process.

Returns:
    A list of chart objects, of class MatrixTable.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.xml_tools.extract_media_xlsx
documentation:
Extracts all charts and images on a given sheet of an XLSX file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the local file.
    page_idx: Index of the sheet to process.

Returns:
    A tuple containing two lists:
    - List of table/chart objects.
    - List of image objects.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.pptx_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from PPTX file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.pptx_sub_chunking._sort_shapes
documentation:
Sorts the shapes on a slide to emulate a natural reading order.

Overlapping shapes are grouped, and the groups are sorted by increasing distance from their barycenter to the top left corner of the slide.
Then the shapes in each group are sorted recursively, the coordinates of the group replacing those of the slide, etc.
The parameter vertical_bias in insert_in_order gives a stronger weight to the vertical axis than to the horizontal one when computing distances.

Args:
    shapes: List of shapes to sort.
    dimensions: The dimensions of the plane on which to sort the shapes (the slide, or a shape group).

Returns:
    A list of ordered shapes.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.pptx_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_pptx
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a PPTX file.

For PPT & ODP : local file converted to PPTX, subchunks extracted from PPTX.
Keeps a numbered slides structure.
(ex : "Slide 1 : ... ; End of slide 1").

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    extension: File extension.
    function: Function to handle images.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists and a Path:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.
    - Path to the processed PPTX file, different from the initial path if a conversion occured.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.pdf_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from PDF file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.pdf_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_pdf
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a PDF file.

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    status_manager: Optional, special object to manage task progress.
    function: Function to handle images.

Returns:
    A list of SubChunk objects.

modules.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing
documentation: Algorithmic correction/processing of table structures.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._check_if_splitted_cell
documentation: Identifies if the input table contains a single value, in which case it is probably the result of splitting a merged cell during XLSX processing.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._square_format
documentation: If the table is not rectangular, adds empty cells to avoid index errors.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._extract_title
documentation:
A first line that only contains 1 non-empty value (potentially repeated) is the result of python-docx splitting the table title in cells.

Can be spread on multiple lines, hence the iteration.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._detect_unit
documentation: Because of how python-docx splits merged cells, two columns can have the same header, one of them containing the units associated to the other one.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._correct_double
documentation:
A column header being repeated can be the result of a duplicated column.

Handles the case where python-docx sometimes duplicates a column's content with another column's header.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._unit_detection_loop
documentation: First auxiliary function to make merge_first_line_doubles less complex.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._double_correction_loop
documentation: Second auxiliary function to make merge_first_line_doubles less complex.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._merge_first_line_doubles
documentation:
A column header being repeated can also be caused by the first line having been splitted on multiple lines.

This function merges lines/columns according to the situation until no repetition remains on the first line.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._merge_fake_lines
documentation:
Lines are sometimes splitted on multiple lines, which can cause sub-lines to not have a header.

This function merges lines that do not have a header to the previous line.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._find_fake_columns
documentation: Identifies if a column without a header must be merged to the left or to the right.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing._merge_fake_columns
documentation: Identifies columns that do not have a header or are units columns and merges them to the left/right according to the situation.

functions.name: unichunking.tools.table_processing.compact_matrix
documentation:
Global table processing functions, calling all the optional subtasks according to the situation.

All subtasks used for correction of DOCX tables.
Only a few used for other formats.
Reasoning behind some functions explained in their respective docstrings.

Args:
    table: MatrixTable object to process.
    do_check_if_splitted_cell: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to check if the table is a splitted XLSX cell.
    do_square_format: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to add cells to make sure the table is a rectangle (same number of cells for each line/column).
    do_delete_empty_lines: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to delete all empty lines.
    do_delete_empty_columns: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to delete all empty columns.
    do_extract_title: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to try to extract a title from the first line doubles in the table.
    do_merge_first_line_doubles: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to merge lines/columns until there are no doubles on the first line.
    do_merge_fake_lines: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to merge lines that were wrongfully separated.
    do_merge_fake_columns: Boolean marker indicating whether or not to merge columns that only contain units to adjacent columns.

Returns:
    The corrected MatrixTable object.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction
documentation: Subchunks extraction for all the supported types.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.txt_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from TXT/CSV/MD file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.txt_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_txt
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a TXT/MD/CSV file.

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    is_csv: Boolean marker indicating whether file must be read as a CSV table.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.ipynb_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from IPYNB file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.ipynb_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_ipynb
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a IPYNB file.

Keeps a typed-cells structure, with information on the programming language.
(ex : "Cell 1 (Text) : ...", "Cell 2 (Code: python 3.11.7) : ...", "Output 1 of cell 2 : ...", "Output 2 of cell 2 : ...").

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.docx_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from DOCX file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.docx_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_docx
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a DOCX file.

For DOC & ODT : local file converted to DOCX, subchunks extracted from DOCX.
Detects any potential page breaks left by renderers to help with further page computing.

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    extension: File extension.
    function: Function to handle images.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists and a Path:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.
    - Path to the processed DOCX file, different from the initial path if a conversion occured.

modules.name: unichunking.formating.chunking
documentation: Chhunk formating.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.chunking.split_chunks_with_overlap
documentation:
Splits the text allowing some overlapping.

- chunks: les différents chunks extraits du document
- k_min: nombre de tokens minimal par chunk
- k_max: nombre maximal de tokens par chunk
- overlap: nombre de caractères pour l'overlap.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.chunking.default_function
documentation: Default function.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.chunking.build_chunked_pages
documentation: Returns a list of Pages and Chunks objects, once extracted, merged when necessary, cleaned and embedded.

modules.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.xlsx_sub_chunking
documentation: Extract subchunks from XLSX file.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.xlsx_sub_chunking._extract_table_from_sheet
documentation:
Extracts the content of a XLSX sheet in the form of the minimal rectangular area that contains all non-empty cells on the sheet.

Formulae are replaced by their outputs.

Args:
    wb: The Workbook object representing the active file.
    sheet: The Worksheet object representing the active sheet.
    sheet_idx: The index of the active sheet.
    evaluator: The Evaluator object used to compute formulae outputs.

Returns;
    The MatrixTable corresponding to the minimal rectangular area that contains all non-empty cells on the sheet.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.xlsx_sub_chunking._split_xlsx_sheet
documentation:
Identifies the different groups of cells on a XLSX sheet, initially represented by a single table containing all the non-empty cells.

Iteratively removes blank lines/columns on the edges and splits the table along interior blank lines/columns.

Args:
    sheet_table: A MatrixTable object representing the minimal rectangular area that contains all non-empty cells on the sheet.

Returns:
    A list containing a MatrixTable object for each cell group on the XLSX sheet.

functions.name: unichunking.subchunk_extraction.xlsx_sub_chunking.extract_subchunks_xlsx
documentation:
Filetype-specific function : extracts subchunks from a XLSX file.

For XLS & ODS : local file converted to XLSX, subchunks extracted from XLSX.
Keeps a named sheets structure.
(ex : "New sheet 'Sheet1' : ... ; End of sheet 'Sheet1'").

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    extension: File extension.

Returns:
    A tuple containing three lists and a Path:
    - List of subchunks, containing actual text or markers pointing to table/chart/image objects.
    - List of table/chart objects, of class MatrixTable.
    - List of image objects.
    - Path to the processed XLSX file, different from the initial path if a conversion occured.

modules.name: unichunking.formating.subchunking
documentation: Subchunk extraction and formating for any file type.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.subchunking._format_table
documentation:
Splits a table to fit in the k_max format.

Keeps the columns/lines information on all subtables to avoid information loss.

Args:
    table: MatrixTable object to split.
    chunk_size: Size of the current chunk without the table, in tokens.
    k_max: Maximum chunk size, in tokens.
    min_format: Minimum number of lines to keep in a subtable.

Returns:
    A list of strings representing the subtables created.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.subchunking._format_subchunks
documentation:
Processes the items extracted by filetype-specific functions to return SubChunks.

Replaces markers to table/chart/image objects by the corresponding objects.
Imposes k_max format by splitting subchunks of exceeding length.
Handles artifical pagination for file types without clear page definition.

Args:
    subchunks: List of subchhunks extracted by filetype-specific function.
    tables: List of table/chart objects extracted by filetype-specific function.
    images: List of image objects extracted by filetype-specific function.
    k_max: Maximum chunk size, in tokens.
    fake_pages_length: Maximum page size, in tokens, for file types without clear page definition.
    status_manager: Optional, special object to manage task progress.

Returns:
    A list of SubChunk objects.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.subchunking.extract_subchunks
documentation:
Global function that extracts chunks from any document.

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    function: Function to describe images
    k_max: Maximum chunk size, in tokens.
    status_manager: Optional, special object to manage task progress.

Returns:
    A list of SubChunk objects.

modules.name: unichunking.formating
documentation: Functions for handling chunking and pages computing.

modules.name: unichunking.formating.utils
documentation: Useful functions for handling SubChunks and Chunks.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.utils.subchunks_to_chunks
documentation: Convert a SubChunk object to a Chunk object with the same content.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.utils.compute_bbox
documentation: Compute the coordinates of the minimal bounding box for a list of chunks.

modules.name: unichunking.utils.logger
documentation: Basic logger module.

modules.name: unichunking.utils
documentation: Package for the logger.

modules.name: unichunking.formating.pages
documentation: Page computing.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.pages.edit_positions_on_page
documentation:
Adjusts the positions of subelements in Chunk objects to avoid overlaps.

For DOCX : no highlighting because sourcing is not precise enough.

Args:
    chunks_pages: A list of lists where chunks_pages[i][j] is Chunk j on page i.
    extension: File extension.

Returns:
    The same list of lists of Chunk objects, whoses positions where adjusted.

functions.name: unichunking.formating.pages.compute_pages
documentation:
Corrects the pagination of DOCX chunks by comparing the text to the converted PDF.

Args:
    path: Path to the local file.
    chunks: List of Chunk objects.
    num_pages: Number of pages originally found through Page Breaks.
    status_manager: Optional, special object to manage task progress.
    increment_reach: Boolean value (True by default) indicating whether or not to increase the page reach when accumulating chunks on the same page.

Returns:
    A tuple containing an int and a list:
    - The actual number of pages in the document.
    - A list containing the Chunk objects, updated with the correct page numbers.
